verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin with no intrinsic membrane anchor. However, documented evidence shows that under non-baseline cellular states—specifically oncogenic transformation and cellular stress (heat shock, oxidative stress, inflammatory states)—HSPD1 translocates to the plasma membrane and is displayed on the cell surface. Cancer cells exhibit enhanced extramitochondrial HSPD1 localization including cell-surface pools; stressed endothelial cells (cigarette smoke exposure) show HSPD1 release from mitochondria and surface transport. The protein reaches the outer leaflet only under these specific stress/transformation conditions, not constitutively.
